פן תורש — The Targum renders this by דלמא תתמסכן LEST THOU BE IMPOVERISHED, for the verb is of the same root as we find in (1 Samuel 2:7) “The Lord maketh poor (מוריש) and maketh rich”.
פן תורש — The Targum renders this by דלמא תתמסכן LEST THOU BE IMPOVERISHED, for the verb is of the same root as we find in (1 Samuel 2:7) “The Lord maketh poor (מוריש) and maketh rich”.

CHAPTER II. Z Harnah’s song-—The sin of Eli’s sons—Samwel’s minisiry—Eli reproveth his sons—A prophecy against Eli’s house. ATP Hannah prayed, and said, My heart rejoiceth in the Lord, mine horn is exalted in the Lord; my mouth is enlarged over mine enemies; because I rejoiced in thy salvation.

Here is a biblical case where a priesthood leader misunderstands a woman, and she, in dignity, yet with respect, clarifies for him. "And Hannah answered and said, No, my lord, I am a woman of a sorrowful spirit: I have drunk neither wine nor strong drink, but have poured out my soul before the Lord. Count not thine handmaid for a daughter of Belial: for out of the abundance of my complaint and…

The name "Belial" properly describes the worthless and wicked sons of Eli but it does not describe Hannah (see 1 Samuel 1:16 and LDS Bible Dictionary, s.v. "Belial," 620). Belial is often used in the Old Testament to refer to Satan" (see Institute Manuel: Genesis-2 Samuel, 268).
